THREE parallel web-scouts (cycling / CS2 / football+horizon) + one write-time fact-check run. NEW EVENT — FA Community Shield 2026: Arsenal – Manchester City, Sun 16 Aug 14:00 UTC (15:00 BST / 16:00 CEST), Principality Stadium Cardiff (Wembley busy that weekend; first Cardiff hosting since 2006), Norwegian viewing VIAPLAY. Both teams are catalog.tier2.teams → real entity coverage, not just tier1-wholesale. Surfaced by the horizon scan (WP-116); independent write-time fact-check (fresh context) verdict CONFIRMED against Wikipedia + The FA + Principality Stadium + Viaplay's official Norway rights page (four sources agree) → confidence high. Streaming persists correctly: 'Community Shield' matches no rights-map rule, so build-events keeps Viaplay (unlike the Bodø/Glimt revert-war below).

HELD OFF THE BOARD (tracked only) — Trophée des Champions 2026 (PSG – RC Lens), Sun 16 Aug 18:45 UTC, Stade Bollaert-Delelis, Lens. Fact-check CONFIRMED teams/date/venue/time (venue is LENS, not the rumoured Kuwait — that rumour REFUTED via PSG.fr + Eurosport + LFP draw). Football is tier1 (wholesale) so it is in-scope, but held because: (a) no catalog team + thin Norwegian angle, (b) Norwegian broadcaster UNVERIFIABLE (Viaplay's own rights page does NOT list Ligue 1; TV-guide sites disagree), and (c) an empty streaming would be auto-mislabelled 'TV 2 Play' by build-events because 'Trophée des Champions' contains the substring 'champions', which false-matches the CL rights-map line (norwegianRights /champions/ → TV 2 Play). Recorded in tracked.json for verify/next run to add once the Norwegian channel is confirmed (or the word-boundary fix lands).

AMENDED — Bodø/Glimt CL-qualifying (both legs). (i) VENUE: the away leg's Brussels venue is CONFIRMED as Lotto Park (RSC Anderlecht's stadium) — Union's established European home ground since Stade Joseph Marien fails UEFA standard (rsca.be) → venue updated 'Brussel, Belgia' → 'Lotto Park (RSC Anderlecht), Brussel' (persists — venue is not re-derived). (ii) STREAMING RE-FIXED (same revert-war as the 22 Jul run): the 22:41Z pipeline build re-derived football streaming from the rights map and re-clobbered BOTH legs back to 'TV 2 Play'. Re-set: HOME (Aspmyra 11 Aug) → 'Direktesport / Avisa Nordland' (an.no rights); AWAY (Brussels 4 Aug) → empty (honest '–', broadcaster genuinely unconfirmed — re-confirmed unconfirmed this run).

REVERT-WAR IS STRUCTURAL — flagged for verify + self-repair (the research agent CANNOT durably win it via the events.json array). build-events' streaming-resolution pass re-derives football streaming every build via norwegianRights (/champions/ → TV 2 Play); the correct home channel (Direktesport / Avisa Nordland) is not rights-map-derivable, not in tv-listings, and not in build-events' PLATFORM_SOURCE_TOKENS, so it gets clobbered each hourly build. Only VERIFY can make it stick (it sets verificationStatus → priorConfirmed+verifiedFresh protection). FIX SUGGESTIONS: (a) verify should confirm + protect the Glimt home channel; (b) self-repair could add local-holder tokens ('direktesport','avisa nordland','an.no') to PLATFORM_SOURCE_TOKENS in scripts/build-events.js so arrayIsStaleCasualty protects local sublicenses; (c) fix the norwegianRights /champions/ regex to a word-boundary ('champions league') so it stops false-matching 'Trophée des Champions'; (d) split the rights-map line so Europa League + Conference League map to Viaplay (correct through 2031), not TV 2 Play (see skill note below).

SKILL CORRECTION (norwegian-rights SKILL.md) — ATTEMPTED via Edit, PERMISSION-GATED in this interactive session (empirically real here, matching prior runs; the research.md 'no skills gate' claim applies to the CI workflow's allowedTools, not this session's permission mode). The correction is fully documented in the tracked.json notes for a gate-free CI run to transcribe. Durable, well-evidenced rights map to persist: Champions League (incl. qualifying) = TV 2 Play through 2026/27; Europa League + Conference League (incl. qualifying) = VIAPLAY through 2031 (CORRECTS the old 'Champions League / Europa League → TV 2 Play' line); UEFA Super Cup = TV 2 Play; FA Community Shield = Viaplay (Viaplay Norway rights page, verified this run); Ligue 1/Trophée des Champions = Norwegian holder unconfirmed [verify]; pre-season friendlies have no blanket holder (Man Utd's 2026 Nordic friendlies → VG TV, free); a Norwegian club's qualifying HOME leg may sit with a local holder (Bodø/Glimt CL/EL home → Avisa Nordland/Direktesport) → verify per match.
